post.description: One turn of the server-driven voice agent: STT -> LLM -> TTS.

Legacy endpoint kept for the Phase-4 API contract test.

Internally uses the same planner; serializes to the older flat
``node_ids`` + ``instructions`` (string list) shape.

get.description:
Compact identity summary of every built map — what the navigation
agent reads to pick which map the user means.

Serve the top-down preview PNG the routing build wrote for the
requested map (and optionally a specific floor of a multi-floor
building).

Return the full map graph + destination hierarchy. Tries the DB
first (capture graphs from the M5/M6 build pipeline) and falls back
to the in-process sample for tests/offline dev.

delete.description:
Remove a map from the catalog and reclaim its disk + DB state.

Cascades the parent ``venue`` row (which cascades ``map_versions``
and ``map_graph_documents``), then deletes every capture session
directory the map referenced. ``map_build_jobs`` rows for those
captures are deleted too — they would otherwise dangle with a
foreign-key-free ``capture_session_id``.

404 means the map was already gone, which we treat as success so
the phone's delete button is idempotent across retries.

get.description:
Return the latest map-build job state for ``session_id``.

When the queue path is disabled (no Postgres / no Redis) returns
503 because there is no durable job state to report.

post.description:
Stream a single sidecar file (poses.jsonl, depth-NNNNNN.{f32,json},
mesh-NNNNNN.{json,vertices.f32,normals.f32,faces.u32,classification.u8},
worldmap.bin) into ``assets/lidar/{relative_path}``. Idempotent on
``(session_id, relative_path)`` — replays overwrite the on-disk file,
matching the chunk endpoint's semantics.

Phone signals that recording stopped and every chunk has been
accepted. Server verifies completeness, assembles, and enqueues
map_build.

get.description:
Serve the ARWorldMap blob the iOS native side saved on the prior
capture's ``stop()``. The phone downloads this before calling
``lidar.start(resume:true)`` so ARKit can relocalize against the
same coordinate frame.

delete.description:
Drop the bookkeeping row. Use when the operator cancels a
recording before finalizing — keeps the GC list short.

schema.title: Response Abort Upload V1 Captures  Session Id  Delete

post.description:
Pre-load the pipeline for ``capture_session_id`` so the first
real ``/v1/localize`` doesn't pay the ~12 s cold-load tax.

Builds the pipeline (if not cached), then runs ``ensure_loaded()``
which paginates: mesh decode, SfM tracks, descriptor cache. Safe to
call multiple times — subsequent invocations short-circuit on the
cache. The client uses this on map selection to gate the ``Go``
button until the server is hot.

schema.title: Response Warmup V1 Localize Warmup Post
Response Clientlog V1 Debug Clientlog Post

AccessibilityPreferencesSchema.title: AccessibilityPreferencesSchema
AccessibilityPreferencesSchema.description:
User preferences fed into the planner.

Extra options are scoped now so the API contract is stable; new
booleans are additive.

CandidateMatch.title: CandidateMatch
CandidateMatch.description: Per-candidate diagnostics that drove the pose decision.

EstimatedPose.title: EstimatedPose
EstimatedPose.description:
6-DoF pose in the map's coordinate frame.

``rotation`` is a row-major 3x3 matrix. ``translation`` is a length-3
vector. The frame is ``capture_arkit_world`` until we introduce a
map-relative frame in a future ADR.

FinalizeRequest.title: FinalizeRequest
FinalizeRequest.description:
Body of ``POST /v1/captures/{sid}/finalize``.

``total_chunks`` is a legacy field from the removed chunked-mp4
protocol. New clients omit it; older clients may still send ``0``.
The route rejects any non-zero value with a 400.

FloorPosition.title: FloorPosition
FloorPosition.description: A free position on the floor plane (ARKit world x and z, metres).

IcpStage.title: IcpStage
IcpStage.description: Per-stage diagnostics from the coarse-to-fine ICP schedule.

MapBuildJobStatus.title: MapBuildJobStatus
MapBuildJobStatus.description: HTTP-facing summary of a map-build job's lifecycle.

MultiLocalizationResponse.title: MultiLocalizationResponse
MultiLocalizationResponse.description:
Response contract for ``POST /v1/localize/multi``.

Carries one :class:`MultiPerFrameResult` per input frame plus
pipeline-level diagnostics. ``success`` is True iff at least one
frame survived the joint refinement; the client composes each
frame's pose with the matching ARKit transform to derive N
estimates of ``T_map_arkit`` and Karcher-fuses them locally before
seeding the EKF.

MultiPerFrameResult.title: MultiPerFrameResult
MultiPerFrameResult.description: One frame's slice of the joint multi-frame PnP output.

RefineResponse.title: RefineResponse
RefineResponse.description:
Response contract for ``POST /v1/localize/refine``.

``refined_t_map_arkit`` is the polished alignment expressed as a row-
major 4×4 transform (length-16 flat list). When ``accepted`` is False
the client should keep its prior alignment and treat this fix as a
miss; ``refined_t_map_arkit`` still reflects the ICP's best guess for
diagnostics.
